title: selaginella lewalleana
intro: Selaginella lewalleana, also known as the resin spikemoss, is a species of spore-producing plant in the genus Selaginella. It is a type of evergreen, rhizomatous plant that is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The medicinal properties of Selaginella lewalleana include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and protecting against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
